top_stocks.description:
• Alamos Gold is a gold producer operating in Canada and Mexico.
• Market cap is approximately US$20.1b.
• Strong market position driven by significant reserve growth at Island Gold and Magino mines.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• Reported Q4 2025 sales of US$575.3m and net income of US$434.9m.
• Increased quarterly dividend by 60% to US$0.04 per share.
• Full-year 2025 net income reached US$885.8m with Island Gold reserves growing 125% to 5.1 million ounces.
• Management targets around 1 million ounces of annual production by 2030.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E of 24.78 is justified by a PEG of 0.41, indicating the stock is undervalued relative to its growth potential; Simply Wall St analysis suggests fair value is significantly higher than current price.
• Profitability: High ROE (22.06%) and ROIC (19.02%) indicate efficient use of equity and capital, typical of a quality gold producer.
• Growth: Low PEG signals that earnings growth expectations are more favorable than the market pricing implies, supported by a solid dividend increase and reserve expansion.
• Quality: Investor Score of 100 indicates a strong fit for quality growth criteria, with analyst estimates forecasting strong future cash generation.

top_stocks.description:
• Micron Technology is a global leader in semiconductor manufacturing, specializing in memory and storage products.
• Classified as a large-cap technology company within the hardware and semiconductors sector.
• Holds a competitive advantage in DRAM and NAND technologies, crucial for data centers and consumer electronics.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• Company maintains a P/E ratio of 38.1 with a PEG of 0.1, indicating strong growth relative to valuation.
• ROE stands at 22.55% and ROIC at 16.85%, reflecting high operational efficiency.
• Profitability margins remain healthy at 28.15%, supporting the overall investment profile.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E of 38.1 is reasonable for a leading tech stock, compounded by a very low PEG of 0.1, suggesting the stock is trading at a significant discount to its actual growth rate.
• Profitability: Exceptional ROE and ROIC suggest the company generates substantial returns on the capital invested by shareholders.
• Growth: Despite modest near-term EPS growth (Next Y: 0.38%), the low PEG ratio suggests high quality growth in the medium to long term.
• Quality: Investor Score of 100 points to a top-tier selection for growth investors, capitalizing on the semiconductor cycle.

top_stocks.description:
• Franco-Nevada is a gold royalty and streaming company, financing mining operations in exchange for rights to a percentage of future production.
• Classified as a mid-to-large-cap company in the materials/mining finance sector.
• Market position is secured by extensive diversified portfolio of gold, silver, and oil and gas royalties.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• Traded at a premium P/E of 54.94, supported by a PEG of 0.69, indicating reasonable valuation expectations for its sector.
• ROE stands at 14.17% with an ROIC of 13.13%, signaling strong cash generation from a passive investment model.
• Profit Margin is robust at 59.51%, making it one of the highest in the group.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E of 54.94 reflects a premium for safety, yet the PEG of 0.69 suggests the growth profile still justifies the valuation compared to peers.
• Profitability: Extremely high profit margin (59.51%) demonstrates the efficiency of the royalty model, generating high cash flow with minimal operational costs.
• Growth: EPS growth rates are moderate (~0.5% annually), characteristic of a mature, income-focused royalty firm rather than a high-growth equities play.
• Quality: Investor Score of 90 suggests a defensive quality selection, balancing stable returns with a solid dividend profile.

top_stocks.description:
• SSR Mining is a Canadian-based intermediate gold mining company focused on operations in Canada, Argentina, Turkey, and the United States.
• Classified as a mid-cap mining company within the materials sector.
• Competitive advantage is derived from its diversified asset portfolio and disciplined approach to production ramp-up.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• Currently trading at a low P/E of 18.2, coupled with a PEG of 0.24, suggesting strong undervaluation.
• ROE and ROIC are solid at 11.97% and 10.83% respectively, indicating operational efficiency typical of tier-one mid-tier miners.
• Profit margin of 24.28% provides a healthy operating buffer.
top_stocks.why_selected: • Valuation: Attractive valuation metrics with a P
